text.title: Swipe File Tweet Hooks Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Swipe File Tweet Hooks application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every entry MUST carry a hook-shape tag from a closed taxonomy; untagged entries are rejected.

rationale.source: Justin Welsh — Content OS hook database

rule.rationale: Untagged swipe files are noise; tags make the file queryable.

rule.statement: Every entry MUST cite source_url + author_handle; orphaned hooks are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without source the file is a plagiarism risk; attribution makes adaptation legitimate.

rule.statement: Every entry MUST carry baseline impressions or engagement number from the source; uncalibrated hooks are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without baseline the operator can't tell whether the hook is a real winner or just memorable.

rule.statement: Operator MUST declare attribution policy (always-cite OR adapt-credit-on-similarity); silent verbatim copying is rejected.
rule.rationale: Plagiarism erodes trust on X faster than slow growth; explicit policy keeps the operator honest.

rule.statement: Swipe file MUST contain ≥20 entries before first use; thin files produce monotone output.

rationale.source: Justin Welsh — Content OS hook database

rule.rationale: <20 entries causes hook-shape repetition within 4 weeks of use.
